name: McDaniel for Congress
subject: On the Trail with Chris: Tupelo, Lee County, and North Mississippi
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Friends of Chris McDaniel
body:
May has already been a busy month as I continue to travel the state. Everywhere I go, I see that the people of Mississippi are fired up for November and eager to show the establishment in Washington that we aren’t going to accept another one of their hand-picked politicians to represent us. | | | | [ ](http://mcdanielforsenate.cmail19.com/t/d-l-bluhuyk-ydllhlurty-r/)
---
Peter,
May has already been a busy month as I continue to travel the state.
Everywhere I go, I see that the people of Mississippi are fired up for November and eager to show the establishment in Washington that we aren’t going to accept another one of their hand-picked politicians to represent us.
I look forward to meeting with supporters like you and as many voters as possible.
Chris
**On the Trail with Chris**
**LEE COUNTY REPUBLICAN CLUB**
On Monday Chris spoke at the Lee County Republican Club, and met with conservatives who are energized and ready to send a Mississippi bulldog to the Senate!

**TUPELO BBQ**
On Tuesday, Chris met with a great group of supporters at the Double Barrel Steakhouse in Tupelo and got to hear directly from them about how much they want to drain the swamp in Washington.
Followed by an appearance at the Jasper County Farm Bureau that evening.
* * *
**Chris on the Trail Last Week:**
**WIGGINS - Sunday, May 6**
We love Stone County! Chris spoke to an incredible congregation at Red Creek Baptist Church in Wiggins.
\----------------
**GRENADA - Monday, May 7**
Chris met with a passionate crowd in Grenada. Mississippians are fired up!
\----------------
**NATCHEZ - Tuesday, May 8**
Thank you to The Borum family of Natchez for a great evening in a beautiful corner of our state!
\----------------
**CLEVELAND - Thursday, May 10**
We love the Delta! Thanks to Doctor Wallace Weatherly and Mr. Bill Dossett for hosting us in Cleveland!
\----------------
**IUKA - Friday, May 11**
We had an incredible week in North Mississippi and capped it off in Iuka.
* * *

name: Amy Klobuchar
subject: Wall Street Journal: "Drug Industry Relieved"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Klobuchar for Minnesota
body:
Dear friend,

After the President's speech laying out his so-called plan on lowering prescription drug prices, a headline in the Wall Street Journal read "Drug Industry Relieved by Price Proposal."

[](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=NMsvQKAF3CG3iA%2F0T5iK%2BiRvuw3YqKNL)
---

**And the proof? After the President 's announcement, Pharma stocks actually went up!**

How about some relief for consumers, for families of kids with cancer struggling to pay for treatment, and for seniors rationing insulin?

I say it's time to pass my bill to lift the ban on negotiating drugs prices for Medicare Part D. I think 41 million seniors could drive a pretty hard bargain and we should let them do it!

[**I also have several bipartisan bills that are ready to go -- we just need more people in Washington willing to stand up to the drug companies. Sign my petition if you agree.**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=%2BlFf9qPCV8sXmCicikPzw4MkM3U%2Bgmp4)

We can get safe, less expensive drugs from countries like Canada. In Minnesota, we can see Canada from our porch! There's no reason we should pay more than double, on average, what people pay in Canada.

And we should end the outrageous practice known as "pay for delay," where brand-name pharmaceutical companies actually pay their competitors to keep generic versions of their drugs off the market. Stopping these deals could save taxpayers nearly $3 billion dollars in just 10 years.

**If you think it 's time for consumers, families and seniors to get some relief, and not the drug companies, [sign my petition.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=YQCpCpNSor%2B8ofRl%2BqLHsIMkM3U%2Bgmp4)**

It's time to get to work.

Amy

name: Shannon Kane
subject: Reflections from graduation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Hi Peter,

Shannon from Team Spanberger here. I walked with my fellow University of Richmond Spiders last Sunday at our graduation. It was a momentous occasion filled with laughter, tears, and a lot of pride for all that we accomplished as a community over the past few years. Now, I'm looking forward to spending my life committed to public service and social justice.

Unfortunately, like many of my fellow classmates, I am graduating with a decent amount of student loan debt. Over the past 15 years, the price to attend my college nearly doubled. As a country, student loans recently surpassed credit cards as the number one source of debt. We need a strong advocate for students on Capitol Hill from Virginia's 7th District. There is only one candidate in this race who has consistently been there for us: Abigail Spanberger.

I dedicated my senior year to working hard to elect Abigail Spanberger as our next representative. Abby has students' backs, and we need to have hers during this election.

The campaign's last fundraising deadline before the primary is coming up soon. Will you **[join me in donating $20.18](https://act.myngp.com/el/3ctaIICjYXZBduaTG4UZE0ID5APEskmZwjpNp28GCmQ=/dGxzQAJrip-ZNtgwB7dXFBnI9bAkXjY3JeoUYxG9aTo=?refcode=shannon)**? You'll be sending a message about the debt crisis now facing college graduates, and you'll be helping to send a person to Capitol Hill who will truly fight for them.
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/3ctaIICjYXZBduaTG4UZE0ID5APEskmZwjpNp28GCmQ=/dGxzQAJrip-ZNtgwB7dXFBnI9bAkXjY3JeoUYxG9aTo=?refcode=shannon)
Thank you for your support, and I hope to see you around the campaign trail.

Best,

Shannon Kane
_University of Richmond, Westhampton College Class of 2018_

Republicans are trying to use the **2020 Census** to strip power from minority and immigrant communities. [**Will you help us stop them? name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Racing the clock to save net neutrality
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Things are moving fast on net neutrality this month. I wanted to make sure you'd heard all the latest news. **The FCC announced that net neutrality protections will end on June 11th.** That's all the time we have to save those protections and the Internet as we know it. If we lose these protections, the consequences are serious. There is a procedure for Congress to overturn FCC rules -- it's called a CRA vote, or Congressional Review Act vote. The Senate just passed the CRA with bipartisan support. Now it comes to the House. House Republicans are against a CRA that would stop the FCC from implementing their repeal of net neutrality protections. They like to put big corporations above consumers any chance they can. **The future of net neutrality is in our hands.** Speaker Ryan and House Republicans don't listen to much, but they will respond if we can show a strong, concentrated outcry in support of net neutrality. The clock is ticking and we're racing in the fight to save net neutrality. [**_Help us build the pressure on Speaker Ryan. Let him know you support net neutrality protections >>_**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fcf/622b7b5b/1adccc43/307b84b2/3188847079/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180517") Thank you. Debbie Wasserman Schultz

clean_body: | | | Things are moving fast on net neutrality this month. I wanted to make sure you'd heard all the latest news. The FCC announced that net neutrality protections will end on June 11th. That's all the time we have to save those protections and the Internet as we know it. If we lose these protections, the consequences are serious. There is a procedure for Congress to overturn FCC rules -- it's called a CRA vote, or Congressional Review Act vote. The Senate just passed the CRA with bipartisan support. Now it comes to the House. House Republicans are against a CRA that would stop the FCC from implementing their repeal of net neutrality protections. They like to put big corporations above consumers any chance they can. The future of net neutrality is in our hands. Speaker Ryan and House Republicans don't listen to much, but they will respond if we can show a strong, concentrated outcry in support of net neutrality. The clock is ticking and we're racing in the fight to save net neutrality. "" Thank you. Debbie Wasserman Schultz | | | Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress

name: Zephyr Teachout
subject: What I said on MSNBC
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Zephyr for NY
body:
Donald Trump and his team are without a doubt the most corrupt group of people to ever occupy the White House.
#
Peter -
A few nights ago, I went on MSNBC to talk about the way I view the role of New York's next Attorney General at this moment in our history.
At the top of that list: fight against the lawlessness, unconstitutional behavior, and corruption of the Trump administration.
So I want to be clear with you: If I were to run and become New York's next Attorney General, I would actively use the powers of the office to protect the people of our state and country from Donald Trump and his administration.
Yes, Donald Trump and his team are without a doubt the most corrupt group of people to ever occupy the White House. And we will take on that corruption.
But the next AG must also be prepared to fight the impact his administration's policies have on our communities. That means suing Scott Pruitt and the EPA when they threaten our environment, fighting the FCC's rollback of net neutrality, and protecting vulnerable populations targeted by Jeff Sessions' DOJ.
I'm prepared to take on all of them, and if I run, I plan to win.
But make no mistake, if that happens we'll be up against a political and financial elite who don't want me within a mile of the AG's office, and they'll do whatever it takes to stop us. But the truth is, they won't stand a chance if we're in this together. That's what it will take. All of us. Together:
**[Can I count on you to make a $3 donation to our exploratory committee today? I can't do this alone. The only path forward is all of us. Together.](https://zephyr.bsd.net/page/m/4d780fc8/6e822e2f/18dba4c3/41721a5a/1917793980/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA1MjAtYSJ9/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-pardons?refcode=em180520-a")**
I also want to make one more thing clear, since it has been in the news a lot lately.
Donald Trump's capacity to pardon does not include state crimes. So if and when he hands out pardons to his cronies, we must use every option we have to prosecute them under state law.
I strongly support a bill pending in the New York legislature that would ensure that state double jeopardy laws would not inhibit such prosecutions.
That is not just within the Attorney General's power, but in my view, it is her responsibility. No matter who is breaking the law, it is the AG's duty to root out corruption and seek justice for the people.
Thanks for being a part of this. Because I cannot do it alone.
Best,
Zephyr
[DONATE](https://zephyr.bsd.net/page/m/4d780fc8/6e822e2f/18dba4c3/41721a5a/1917793980/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA1MjAtYSJ9/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-pardons?refcode=em180520-a")

body:
FIRST Fox News called Indiana's Senate seat "vulnerable and a potential pick-up in the Senate for the GOP." THEN Donald Trump came to Indiana to campaign against Joe. AND Mike Pence was just in Indian |
---

| | Here's what you need to know, Peter:
> _**FIRST**_ Fox News called Indiana's Senate seat "vulnerable and a potential pick-up in the Senate for the GOP."
>
> _**THEN**_ Donald Trump came to Indiana to campaign against Joe.
>
> **_AND_****Mike Pence was just in Indianapolis to raise money for Joe 's opponent!**
>
> _**NOW**_ the _Washington Post_ is reporting that one of the Republican National Committee's top priorities is organizing to defeat Joe.
**If we 're going to win and keep Joe in the Senate, then _we need to out-organize them_. We're building a massive field campaign that reaches every corner of the state -- will you chip in?** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

I know Joe won't let all the attacks get to him -- he's knows doing what's right for Hoosiers sometimes means he'll have to fight against outside spenders who are trying to buy Indiana's voice in the Senate.

But just because Joe isn't scared doesn't mean I'm not! As Finance Director, it's my responsibility to know where we are on our fundraising goals and to have a plan to meet them. **And let me tell you, all that dark money pouring into Indiana from D.C. is making my job _really hard_.**

Wealthy campaign donors like the Kochs want to get even richer at ordinary working people's expense, so they're _furious_ that Joe wouldn't vote to take access to health care away from Hoosiers and pass the McConnell tax bill. And Karl Rove's cronies have started tearing down Joe, too. They're intent on silencing Joe's independent voice and replacing him with a rubber stamp for _their_ agenda.

**But Joe doesn 't work for any president, political party, or wealthy donor -- he's Indiana's hired help in the Senate, and the only thing he's worried about is fighting for Hoosiers.**

**[Will you pitch in and help us send him back to the Senate so he can keep fighting for us? >>](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2134/7377f838/3f93a57e/51f91f4d/1152924967/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-goporganize?refcode=20180520nd&amount=10")**

Kelly Norton

name: U.S. Senator Tammy Baldwin
subject: I'm asking for your help, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
No ifs, ands, or buts about it -- to have any chance at taking back the Senate this year, we have to win in Wisconsin, Derek.
---
No ifs, ands, or buts about it -- to have any chance at taking back the Senate this year, we have to win in Wisconsin, Derek.

Trump won here in 2016, and Scott Walker's party holds all but one of Wisconsin's major statewide elected offices: Mine. I'm facing a tough re-election this year -- and I need your help to hold on to my seat and give Democrats a chance to flip the Senate.

**Split a $10 donation between my campaign and the DNC to support my re-election and elect Democrats in all 50 states this year.**

**[ DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c4b/4237354739/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/reelect-tammy?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c48/4237354739/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/reelect-tammy?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c49/4237354739/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/reelect-tammy?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c56/4237354739/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/reelect-tammy?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")
[DONATE: $200](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c57/4237354739/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/reelect-tammy?amount=200&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+DONATE+200&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c54/4237354739/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/reelect-tammy?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")
** This election is about choices, Derek -- and nowhere are those choices more clear than in Wisconsin. We get to decide between our vision of health care as a right for all Americans or Paul Ryan's plan to let families go bankrupt in medical emergencies. We get to decide if women control our own medical decisions. We get to decide if lesbian, gay, and transgender people enjoy the same rights as our straight friends. And we get to decide if we want to build the middle class or just let the rich get richer.

This is our democracy, and in a democracy we decide what's possible. The choice is yours: **Should we put in the work to elect Democrats who will fight for our values, or let Republicans like Paul Ryan and Scott Walker keep winning?**

**[Split a $10 donation between my campaign and the DNC today.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522fde/4067b0a/59ba0aab0/7e471c55/4237354739/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/reelect-tammy?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+Split+a++if+consinConsGroup28497++set+ma&utm_campaign=em_20180520_tb_nd&source=em_20180520_tb_nd&refcode=em_20180520_tb_nd")**

I want to continue fighting for our shared values in the Senate, but I won't be able to do that without your help, Derek.

Tammy

Tammy Baldwin
U.S. Senator, Wisconsin

P.S. -- We can't afford to let anyone sit this one out. **[If you're able, split a $10 donation between my campaign and the DNC today.

Peter,

[Please take my quick survey](http://troydowningforussenate.cmail19.com/t/d-l-aihhij-yhdyitiujj-x/) and let me know what your priorities for Montana are.
